text:
One AI video isn't one prompt.

It's a process.

Start with still shots. Piece the frames together — frame one to frame two, add motion, get a clip. Do it five times.

Then stitch the clips into one continuous story in the editor.

That's exactly how we built this on Tech Thursday. No magic. Just the build, in the open.

Watch the full breakdown.

visual.library_hint: vertical screen-recording walking through the AI video build: still shots to frame pairs to five clips, stitched together in the editor

text:
Anthropic kept a model called Mythos under wraps — too powerful to ship.

This week: Fable 5, the first "Mythos-class" release. Agentic coding around 80%.

Knowing what dropped is step one. Building with it is the point.

visual.library_hint: minimal type card on deep black, electric blue accent, Fable 5 / Mythos-class benchmark motif

text:
We're building the thing we keep talking about.

A content engine that drafts tomorrow's posts, lines up the visuals, and then waits for a human to say yes.

The shift everyone's feeling is real: AI is moving from one prompt to agents that keep working while you sleep.

But autonomy without judgment is just noise. So nothing here publishes until a person approves it.

That's the eighth layer — people deciding what the tools actually mean.

Comment "build" if you want the breakdown.

text:
The conversation is over.

AI isn't answering your questions anymore.
It's doing the work.

Claude Fable 5 just dropped. Every major lab is shipping the same message right now:
chat is the baseline.
Agents that plan, execute, fix, and loop — that's the new floor.

This isn't about prompting smarter.
It's about knowing how to build with tools that act.

AI fluency isn't reading about it.
It's showing up where the builds happen.

That's why we do this. 🔵

text:
Be honest.

How many 30-second clips have you watched about AI this week?

On Tech Thursday, someone said it perfectly:
“You can watch 20 clips on the same subject and feel like an expert.
But you just spent 20 minutes on it.”

Depth is the move.
Not more content. More time in the tools.

When did you actually sit down and build something?

Drop it below. 👇

text:
AI isn't waiting for instructions anymore.

We crossed a line this month.

Claude Fable 5. NVIDIA Cosmos 3 — vision, reasoning, and world simulation in a single model. Agents embedding directly into lending workflows, healthcare, procurement. Not as assistants. As actors.

The shift: from chat to task completion.
From tool to teammate.

That's not a feature update. That's a new relationship.

The question isn't whether you use AI.
It's whether you understand it well enough to actually direct it.

AI fluency isn't optional anymore. It's the baseline.

What's one workflow you've handed to an agent this month? Drop it below. 👇

AI is the tool. Culture is the multiplier.

visual.headline: AGENTS ARE THE NEW INTERFACE

text:
We built the thing that builds our content.

Every post you see right now is moving through a content engine we built with GitHub Copilot CLI.

The agent reads the brand guide.
Researches the news.
Writes the copy.
Formats the JSON.
Composes the image prompts.
Queues it for human approval.

All of it — automated. With a gate so nothing ships without a real review. Because the build is ours, and the voice has to stay ours.

That's the agentic shift in practice.
Not theory. Not a demo. The real thing — live, in public.

The code lives on GitHub. We build it week by week on Tech Thursday. Watch it happen. 🔧

Learn in public. Build in public. Shape the future.

visual.headline: WE BUILT THE BUILDER

text:
2am. Sleep-deprived. And he shipped a full website in under an hour.

That's what happens when you lock in with the tools.

At some point during 24 hours straight, it stops feeling like tech and starts feeling like proof. Proof of what's possible when you stop scrolling and actually build.

"Literally anything you can think of can be built."

We broke things. Fixed them. Did it again. ChatGPT. Claude Fable 5. Copilot. The tools don't care what time it is.

Twenty clips on a subject feels like learning. Twenty hours of actually building? That installs something different.

Do the hours.

Full 24-hour AI hackathon recap is on Tech Thursday. Link in bio. 🎙️

No green-room gloss. Just the build in progress.

visual.headline: BUILD IT. BREAK IT. REPEAT.

text:
A billion people use ChatGPT every month.

A billion.

And most of them are asking questions. Getting answers. Consuming.

That's fine. But there's a different move — learning the tools deeply, building with them, shaping what they become. Understanding the system, not just using it.

We can't spend the AI era only scrolling and consuming. We have to learn the tools, build with them, and shape what comes next. That's Layer8Culture.

A billion monthly users.
How many builders?

Which side of that line are you on?

Drop it in the comments. 👇

Build the future. Don't just consume it.

visual.headline: CONSUMERS OR BUILDERS?

text:
AI fluency isn’t just knowing the tools.

It’s knowing the landscape you’re operating in.

Colorado’s AI Act takes effect July 1 — the first U.S. state law requiring companies that deploy high-risk AI systems to actively manage bias, disclose how decisions are made, and protect the people those systems affect.

More states are watching.
More legislation is coming.

This isn’t fear-mongering. It’s the next layer of literacy.

The builders who understand the rules will build with more confidence than those who don’t.

AI fluency was never just about prompts.

That’s Layer8Culture. 🔵

text:
The labs just shipped.

Gemini 3.5 Flash for speed and agentic tasks.
Claude Opus 4.8 for depth and reasoning.
A new tier of capability — across the board.

Every major player is converging on the same direction:
AI that doesn’t just respond. AI that executes, loops, and ships.

This is the moment builders have been waiting for.

More tools. More capability. More access to what used to require a full engineering team.

The field is wide open.

Are you in it yet?

Link in bio for the full breakdown. 🔵

visual.headline: THE FIELD IS OPEN

text:
At 2 in the morning, one of us was falling asleep.

The other was still building.

Shipped a full website in under an hour. First time touching a terminal.

Twenty-four hours. Two builders. One focused sprint.

And afterward — the thing that shifted:

“Literally anything you can think of can be built.”

That’s what deep focus unlocks. Not a tutorial. Not a recap. Time in the tools, side by side.

That’s the model. That’s what we do every Thursday.

Comment “build” if you’ve felt that shift. 👇 🔵

visual.headline: ANYTHING CAN BE BUILT

text:
On Tech Thursday someone said it plainly:

“Building is not something we need to do in a silo.
There’s no need to hoard ideas.”

That landed.

We’re in an era where sharing the build — the process, the early version, the thing that isn’t finished yet — is actually the strategy.

Because ideas only grow when more people see them.

So we’ll ask:

What are you working on that you haven’t shared yet?

Drop it below. 👇

visual.headline: SHARE THE BUILD

text:
Apple made it official at WWDC.

Claude is now an assistant option on your iPhone.
Not a workaround. Not a third-party app.
A system-level choice.

Your AI is becoming as personal as your playlist.

The era where one company decides the AI you use by default is ending.
Now you pick based on how you think, what you build, how you communicate.

That's not just a product update.
That's AI fluency becoming a daily practice.

Know your tools. Choose them on purpose. 🔵

visual.headline: YOUR AI. YOUR CHOICE.
visual.subtext: The personal AI era is here.

text:
The most powerful thing we built into this content engine wasn't a prompt.

It was a skill file.

Skills are behavior contracts for AI agents.
Not: "write me a post."
But: here's the brand voice, the content rules, the image formula, the phrases we never use.

The agent reads it before it does anything.
Output is consistent because the instructions are consistent.

That's the shift from prompt dependency to system design.

Prompts get you one output.
Skills get you a workflow.

Tech Thursday is where we break these builds down live. 🔵

visual.headline: PROMPTS → SKILLS

text:
Building AI video isn't one prompt.
It's a system.

Still frame.
Frame pair.
Motion clip.

Repeat five times.
Stitch in the editor.
You have a story.

We built this live on Tech Thursday — and here's what happened after:
something unlocked.

"Using AI in the right way really energizes you.
You finish one thing and immediately want to fix the next problem."

That's the signal.
When the tools compound your momentum, you stop asking if it's worth learning.

You're already building.

Full session → link in bio. 🔵

visual.headline: BUILD THE SYSTEM

text:
Open-source AI is catching up.

MiniMax M3 just dropped — open-weight, one million token context, outperforming several proprietary APIs on coding and agent tasks.

This matters for builders:
the model monopoly is ending.

You don't have to be locked into one lab.
You don't have to stack subscriptions.
You can run powerful models on your own infrastructure.

The smartest builders we know aren't asking which AI is best.
They're building systems flexible enough to swap the model in.

Stay platform-independent.
Build for portability. 🔵
